title: Product Catalogue Management

activities.name: addProduct (raw fields)
activities.signature: addProduct(String prodName, String prodType, String prodInfo, double prodPrice, int prodQuantity, InputStream prodImage): String
steps: Construct ProductBean(prodId, prodName, prodType, prodInfo, prodPrice, prodQuantity, prodImage)

activities.name: addProduct (ProductBean)
steps: If product.getProdId() == null → generate ID via IDUtil.generateId()
Execute INSERT INTO product VALUES(pid, pname, ptype, pinfo, pprice, pquantity, image)
[k > 0] → status = 'Product Added Successfully with Product Id: {id}'

activities.name: removeProduct
steps: Execute DELETE FROM product WHERE pid=?
→ Also execute DELETE FROM usercart WHERE prodid=? (cascade cart cleanup)

activities.name: updateProduct (with image)
steps: [IDs differ] → return 'Both Products are Different, Updation Failed!'
Execute UPDATE product SET pname,ptype,pinfo,pprice,pquantity,image WHERE pid=?

activities.name: updateProductWithoutImage
steps: [IDs differ] → return 'Both Products are Different, Updation Failed!'
Execute UPDATE product SET pname,ptype,pinfo,pprice,pquantity WHERE pid=? (no image column)
→ Append 'And Mail Send to customers...' to status
[k > 0 AND no restock] → status = 'Product Updated Successfully!'
[k == 0] → status = 'Product Not available in the store!'

activities.name: searchAllProducts
steps: Execute SELECT * FROM product WHERE lower(ptype) LIKE ? OR lower(pname) LIKE ? OR lower(pinfo) LIKE ?
Iterate ResultSet → construct ProductBean for each row (id, name, type, info, price, quantity, imageStream)
Add each ProductBean to products list
[SQLException] → log stack trace, return empty list

activities.name: getProductDetails
steps: Execute SELECT * FROM product WHERE pid=?
[rs.next()] → construct ProductBean (id, name, type, info, price, quantity, imageStream)

activities.name: getImage
steps: Execute SELECT image FROM product WHERE pid=?

activities.name: updateProductPrice
steps: Execute UPDATE product SET pprice=? WHERE pid=?

key_relationships: ProductServiceImpl depends_on IDUtil (ID generation for addProduct)
ProductServiceImpl depends_on DemandServiceImpl (restock demand lookup & removal)
ProductServiceImpl depends_on UserServiceImpl (fetch user first name for email)
ProductServiceImpl depends_on MailMessage (send restock notification emails)
removeProduct cascades DELETE to usercart table
updateProductWithoutImage triggers demand notification workflow when quantity increases
branching_conditions: addProduct(ProductBean): prodId null check → auto-generate ID
updateProduct / updateProductWithoutImage: ID mismatch → early return failure
updateProductWithoutImage: prevQuantity < updatedQuantity → trigger restock email loop
removeProduct: k > 0 → cascade delete from usercart
getProductDetails / getImage: rs.next() → row found vs null return
db_table: product (pid, pname, ptype, pinfo, pprice, pquantity, image)
cascade_table: usercart (prodid FK, cleaned on product removal)
important_notes: updateProductWithoutImage is the most complex flow — it checks quantity restock, queries demand list, sends notification emails, and removes demand records in a loop. addProduct has two overloads: raw-field version delegates to ProductBean version. All methods follow open-connection → execute → close pattern via DBUtil.
